title: impatiens balsamina var. rosea
intro: Impatiens balsamina var. rosea, also known as the rose balsam or rose garden balsam, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the balsam family and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Impatiens balsamina var. rosea include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin conditions. Impatiens balsamina var. rosea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its flowers can also be used as a natural dye for fabric.
